sanskrit:
अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते।
तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्॥
transliteration: ananyāś cintayanto māṁ ye janāḥ paryupāsate | teṣāṁ nityābhiyuktānāṁ yoga-kṣemaṁ vahāmy aham ||
literal_meaning: Those who, without deviation, think of Me and worship Me—always engaged—I personally carry their yoga (what they need) and kṣema (what they have).
contextual_meaning: When attention is single-pointedly placed on an internalized sacred presence, the psyche reorganizes itself; resources arrive, threats recede, and the self-system is held by an intelligence larger than ego.
psychological_state: Secure attachment to an internalized representation of the divine; reduction in amygdala reactivity and increase in default-mode-network coherence.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out caregiver sits in the hospital chapel, whispers the same name over and over, and suddenly notices her shoulders drop, the next breath arrive, and a subtle certainty that ‘somehow it will be handled.’
anchor_text: Keep your mind on the Divine and everything you need will be brought to you.
